findings.code: query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id='" + user_id + "'"
findings.description: SQL query is constructed using untrusted user input.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

findings.description: ORM/HQL query is constructed using untrusted user input.
findings.recommendation: Use safe ORM parameters instead of string concatenation.

findings.description: User input is passed directly to an operating system command.
findings.recommendation: Avoid passing user input directly to system commands.

findings.description: MongoDB query is constructed using untrusted user input.
findings.recommendation: Validate input and build MongoDB filters using allowed fields and safe operators only.

findings.code: query = "SELECT * FROM users WHERE id='" + user_id + "'"
findings.description: SQL query is constructed using untrusted user input.
findings.recommendation: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements.

findings.description: ORM/HQL query is constructed using untrusted user input.
findings.recommendation: Use safe ORM parameters instead of string concatenation.

findings.description: User input is passed directly to an operating system command.
findings.recommendation: Avoid passing user input directly to system commands.

findings.description: MongoDB query is constructed using untrusted user input.
findings.recommendation: Validate input and build MongoDB filters using allowed fields and safe operators only.

findings.description: MongoDB query uses untrusted input with MongoDB operators.
findings.recommendation: Do not allow user-controlled MongoDB operators such as $ne, $where, $regex, or $or.

recommendations: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements for SQL queries.
Use safe ORM parameters instead of raw query construction.
Avoid passing user input directly to operating system commands.
Validate MongoDB filters and block user-controlled operators such as $ne, $where and $regex.
